events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need assess field condition anchor bolts field-bent to fit. Reference AISC 303 and OSHA 1926 Subpart R. Determine out of tolerance and why. Need produce JSON shape with all fields.

Need understand trade: anchor bolts field-bent to fit. In structural steel erection, anchor rods (anchor bolts) must be set per drawings; field bending of anchor rods generally prohibited. AISC 303 (Code of Standard Practice for Steel Buildings and Bridges) requires anchor rods set to tolerances and not bent. AISC 303-16 Section 7.5.1? Anchor rods shall be set within tolerances; no field bending unless approved by engineer. OSHA 1926.755(a)(2) anchor bolts (anchor rods) shall not be repaired, replaced, or modified without approval of project structural engineer of record. Field bending to fit is non-compliant. Work rough-complete. Decision fail because dangerous/non-compliant. Risk high or critical? Anchor bolt integrity compromised; could affect column base stability and load capacity. In industrial site, high. We'll set high.

Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete maybe 80 rough-complete. value: 0 because no numeric reading asked? But task says estimate measurable condition. Could use value to represent estimated out-of-tolerance e.g., 1 (bent) maybe not. Since schema numeric reading or computed quantity; not needed. Could set 0. confidence 0.9. event_time 0. rate 0. order empty. workable empty. findings list. actions: remove/replace, consult engineer, NDT. references: AISC 303, OSHA 1926.755. rationale: field bending not permitted unless engineered approval; cold bending induces cracks, reduces capacity, violates tolerances; must be replaced/repaired by engineer.

Need raw JSON no markdown.

events.label: response
events.text: {"decision": "fail", "risk": "high", "work_stage": "rough-complete", "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"], "percent_complete": 85, "value": 0, "sound_source": "", "confidence": 0.9, "event_time": 0.0, "rate": 0.0, "order": [], "workable": [], "findings": ["Anchor bolts were field-bent to align with column base plates.", "Field bending of anchor rods is not permitted without written approval from the Engineer of Record.", "Cold bending can initiate cracks, reduce tensile/anchorage capacity, and prevent proper bearing/nut engagement.", "Installation violates anchor-rod placement tolerances and base connection requirements."], "actions": ["Stop work on affected column/base connection until resolved.", "Notify the structural Engineer of Record and site inspector; do not conceal or accept as-is.", "Replace or repair anchor rods per an engineer-approved repair detail; do not re-bend in the field without authorization.", "Verify new/repaired anchor rods meet layout, plumbness, projection, and thread-projection tolerances before column placement.", "Document NDT/corrective action and obtain inspection sign-off before resuming erection."], "rationale": "AISC 303 requires anchor rods to be placed within specified tolerances and prohibits unauthorized field modification. OSHA 1926.755(a)(2) specifically forbids repairing, replacing, or modifying anchor rods without the approval of the project structural Engineer of Record. Field-bending anchor bolts to fit is a non-compliant, defective condition that compromises the structural connection and erection safety.", "references": ["AISC 303 Code of Standard Practice for Steel Buildings and Bridges", "OSHA 1926.755(a)(2)"]}
